news.summary: Workhuman®, a top employee recognition firm, concluded its tenth Workhuman Live event in Denver, bringing together thousands of HR executives to discuss reshaping workplace cultures. This year's event urged attendees to move beyond just accepting change, and instead take charge in driving it. The focus was on actively shaping the future of work culture rather than playing a passive role. There was a call to action for HR leaders to embrace change and become architects of their organizations' futures.
news.headline: Workhuman concludes 10th Workhuman Live conference in Denver, announces 2026 event in Orlando, FL. Theme: reimagining workplace culture.

news.summary: In Miami, an acquisition deal was revealed by I Squared Capital, a prominent global infrastructure investment manager, in partnership with MPLX LP (NYSE: MPLX) and Enbridge Inc. (NYSE: ENB). The agreement involves the purchase of a notable equity stake in the Matterhorn Express Pipeline (MXP), which functions as a crucial 2.5 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) natural gas pipeline linking the Permian Basin to vital Texas demand hubs and U.S. Gulf Coast LNG export terminals. This strategic investment showcases the commitment to expanding infrastructure resources.
news.headline: I Squared Capital to acquire equity in Matterhorn Express Pipeline, a 2.5 Bcf/d gas pipeline linking Permian Basin to Texas and Gulf Coast - a strategic move.

news.summary: CoreWeave Inc (NASDAQ: CRWV) exceeded sales expectations for the first quarter, announcing impressive revenue of $981.63 million. This outperformed analyst predictions of $859.77 million. Despite this success, the company reported an adjusted loss of $1.49 per share for the quarter. Year-over-year, total revenue witnessed a substantial 420% increase. Additionally, CoreWeave disclosed a significant revenue backlog of $25.9 billion at the end of the quarter. The company expressed satisfaction with the strong financial performance in the first quarter, marking the beginning of a successful year. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: CoreWeave (CRWV) exceeds Q1 sales estimates with revenue of $981.63M, up 420% YoY, ending quarter with $25.9B backlog.

news.summary: Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. will announce its fourth-quarter financial results on May 15 after the market closes. Analysts predict a loss of 5 cents per share for the New York-based company. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $1.55 billion, compared to $1.35 billion the previous year. On March 3, Rockstar Games, a subsidiary of Take-Two, disclosed the acquisition of Sydney-based Video Games Deluxe, which will be rebranded as Rockstar Australia. Take-Two's stock rose by 1.5% to close at $229.50 on Wednesday. For the latest analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga, where ratings can be sorted by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, rating change, and other criteria.
news.headline: Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ: TTWO) to report Q4 earnings on May 15, revenue expected at $1.55 billion. Rockstar Games acquiring Video Games Deluxe. Stock gained 1.5% to close at $229.50. More info on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Deere & Co. (NYSE: DE) stocks are on the rise following the release of their second-quarter 2025 results, which surpassed expectations. The company saw a 16% year-over-year decline in quarterly net sales and revenue, totaling $12.76 billion, surpassing the projected $10.79 billion. Net sales for the quarter were $11.17 billion, with a total of $17.98 billion for the first half, down from $13.61 billion and $24.10 billion from the previous year. Deere's earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $6.64, lower than the $8.23 from the prior year but ahead of the consensus of $5.59. Production & Precision Agriculture net sales dropped by 21% YoY to $5.23 billion, while Small Agriculture & Turf net sales decreased by 6.5% to $2.994 billion. Construction & Forestry revenue also fell by 23% to $2.947 billion due to reduced shipment volumes.
news.headline: Deere & Co. beats Q2 expectations despite sales decline, with net sales at $12.76B and EPS of $6.64. Shares rise.

news.headline: Q2i's PARCA app helps those on probation/parole with tech coaching, incentives. gaining traction with more agencies.

news.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield is the interest rate the US government pays to borrow money for 10 years. It's currently around 4.37%-4.39%. It impacts global financial markets, influencing stocks, currencies, borrowing costs, and emerging economies. The yield affects crypto by influencing investor behavior and market sentiment. Rising yields can lead to lower demand for riskier crypto yields. Stablecoins like USDC are connected to Treasury yields and regulatory frameworks. DeFi platforms can be impacted by rising Treasury yields. Tokenized Treasurys have emerged in the crypto space, with a total value of $6.5 billion in 2025. Understanding Treasury yields can help crypto investors navigate market shifts and make informed decisions.
news.headline: 10-year Treasury yield, key for crypto & stablecoins. Yield at 4.37-4.39% in '25, impacts global markets. Rising yield affects crypto yields & stablecoins.

news.summary: Ethereum (ETH) has seen a 4.50% increase in its trading value, now standing at $2,610.05. Over the past seven days, ETH has surged by 42.19%, with a monthly gain of over 55%. This increase is attributed to various factors, including $560 million in institutional investments, technical advancements like Pectra, and anticipation surrounding the potential approval of an SEC spot ETF. This positive outlook has reignited optimism in the crypto market. The recent surge in ETH accumulation by large investors, or "whales," is noteworthy. Reports indicate that Abraxas Capital acquired more than $477 million worth of ETH in the last week, purchasing approximately 211,000 tokens. Additionally, Grayscale added $83 million worth of Ethereum to its portfolio. news.summary: In April, consumer prices in the U.S. saw a slower decrease than predicted, despite the introduction of new tariffs by President Donald Trump. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) showed a 2.3% increase from a year earlier, below the anticipated 2.4% and the previous rate. The monthly rise was 0.2%, recovering from a 0.1% drop but falling short of the expected 0.3%. The core CPI, excluding food and energy prices, maintained an annual increase of 2.8%, in line with both previous and expected figures. On a monthly basis, it rose by 0.2%, up from the 0.1% rise in March but below the 0.3% forecast. These figures suggest that the impact of recent tariffs on living costs was not as immediate as anticipated.
news.headline: US inflation eases in April with CPI at 2.3% (missed 2.4% forecast), core CPI at 2.8%, monthly gain of 0.2%. Tariffs not affecting prices significantly.

news.summary: Wall Street cautiously rose at the opening on Tuesday following April's inflation data. Consumer prices in April increased by 2.3% year over year, slightly lower than the predicted 2.4%, and down from the previous month's 2.4%. Monthly prices saw a 0.2% rise, bouncing back from March's 0.1% drop but missing the expected 0.3% increase. Core inflation, excluding food and energy, remained at 2.8% annually, in line with forecasts and March. Monthly core prices increased by 0.2%, up from March's 0.1% but below the expected 0.3%. Shelter saw a 0.3% rise in April, contributing over half of the overall Consumer Price Index increase. Some categories experienced price drops such as airline fares, used cars and trucks, communication services.
news.headline: Wall Street rises as inflation cools: April's consumer prices up 2.3% YoY, core inflation at 2.8%. Nasdaq 100 hits 10-week high.

news.headline: OTC Markets Group welcomes Consensus Mining & Seigniorage Corporation to OTCQX, trading 12,000 securities.

news.summary: Renowned economist Nouriel Roubini, dubbed "Dr. Doom" for his accurate prediction of the 2008 financial crisis, criticized President Donald Trump for backing down in the trade dispute with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Roubini stated that Trump failed to secure significant concessions from China, resulting in a clear win for Xi. He cautioned that further escalation would have devastating economic effects, including soaring U.S. inflation, supply chain disruptions, and a global recession. Roubini warned that such repercussions would have jeopardized the GOP and Trump's ambitions by the 2026 midterm elections. He suggested that minor concessions from China could still be possible. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Economist Nouriel Roubini criticizes Trump for backing down in trade war with China, calls Xi Jinping the clear winner. Severe consequences avoided due to tariff rollback.

news.summary: Goldman Sachs has adjusted its outlook for the S&P 500 Index, with equity strategist David J. Kostin raising the 12-month target to 6,500, up from 6,200. The three-month target has also been increased from 5,700 to 5,900, and the six-month view is now at 6,100, compared to the previous 5,900. Kostin attributed these revisions to factors such as lower tariff rates, improved economic growth, and reduced recession risk. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) rose 0.4% to 5,860 on Tuesday, reflecting a 21% recovery since April's lows, reaching levels last seen in early March. Key drivers of this rally include...
news.headline: Goldman Sachs raises S&P 500 target to 6,500, trims recession odds due to lower tariff rates and better growth, as index climbs to 5,860.

news.summary: Dynatrace, Inc. (NYSE: DT) exceeded expectations in their fourth-quarter earnings report, with a 17% year-over-year revenue growth reaching $445.17 million. This surpassed the analyst consensus of $434.78 million. The adjusted earnings per share also showed improvement at 33 cents, up from the anticipated 30 cents. Looking ahead, Dynatrace provided a robust guidance forecasting revenue between $465.00 million and $470.00 million, higher than the consensus estimate of $454.51 million. The expected adjusted earnings per share for the upcoming period are $0.37 to $0.38, surpassing the estimated 0.35 cents. For the fiscal year 2026, Dynatrace expects revenue in the range of $1.950 billion to $1.965 billion in comparison to the analyst consensus of $1.940 billion. Additionally, they anticipate an Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) between $1.975 billion and $1.990 billion. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Dynatrace (NYSE: DT) exceeds Q4 expectations, revenue hits $445.17M vs $434.78M estimate. Raises 2026 forecast to $1.950B-$1.965B.

news.summary: A recent survey from Savanta demonstrates the complex nature of American sentiments leading up to Memorial Day 2025. As the nation prepares to celebrate, findings reveal a divided populace grappling with a mix of traditional values, financial burdens, and a yearning for greater purpose. While individuals gear up for holiday festivities, a sizable portion are facing economic constraints and reconsidering their priorities. The study sheds light on the multifaceted mindset of the country, showcasing the range of perspectives and challenges being navigated as the holiday approaches.
news.headline: Americans are conflicted about Memorial Day 2025, with a Savanta survey showing financial stress and desire for deeper meaning.

news.summary: In Wilmington, North Carolina, New Leaf Builders has reached a significant achievement by completing its 100th home in the Riverlights community. The milestone represents a key point in the partnership between the company and one of the rapidly expanding master-planned neighborhoods in the area. Since becoming part of the Riverlights neighborhood in January 2022, New Leaf Builders has contributed to the community's development by offering a collection of well-designed, Lowcountry-inspired residences that capture the architectural allure and coastal living essence.
news.headline: New Leaf Builders completes its 100th home in Riverlights, Wilmington marking a significant milestone in the partnership.
